final_prompt.prompt:
Create an original, non-infringing Xiaohongshu cover image announcing GPT-image-2 on the Lovart platform, in the restrained editorial spirit of Paula Scher's typographic poster work.

Style/medium: high-end magazine editorial cover with bold typographic architecture, the kind that commands a newsstand glance
Composition: diagonal composition — key visual elements and text blocks arranged along a strong diagonal axis cutting from upper-left to lower-right, creating asymmetric tension
Subject: the announcement that GPT-image-2 is now live on Lovart, expressed through typographic hierarchy and graphic accent shapes
Scene/backdrop: clean, minimal studio ground — negative space as a deliberate design choice, not emptiness
Lighting/mood: the cool confidence of a flagship editorial launch — no warmth, no softness, just presence
Color palette: high-contrast accent color scheme — deep near-black ground in sharp dialogue with one saturated accent (electric cobalt or acid yellow), the tension between the two carrying the entire visual weight
Materials/textures: matte ink on coated stock, crisp edges, zero noise
Text (verbatim): GPT-image-2   Lovart
Constraints: Xiaohongshu cover ratio (portrait 3:4), both verbatim text strings must be legible and prominent, diagonal composition must be clearly readable, high-contrast accent color must dominate
Avoid: soft gradients, warm lifestyle tones, decorative flourishes, generic tech-startup blue, centered symmetrical layout
